title: Flowise DocumentStore create endpoint mass-assignment / IDOR via client-supplied primary key (UPSERT)
description: The POST /api/v1/document-store create flow maps the entire request body to a DocumentStore entity (DocumentStoreDTO.toEntity(body) in the controller) with no DTO allowlist, then createDocumentStore() in packages/server/src/services/documentstore/index.ts calls repository.create(newDocumentStore) followed by repository.save(documentStore). DocumentStore.id is a globally-unique @PrimaryGeneratedColumn('uuid') that is not composite with workspaceId, and because TypeORM's save() performs an UPDATE when the primary key already exists, accepting a client-supplied id turns the create endpoint into an implicit UPSERT. An authenticated user who knows or enumerates a DocumentStore UUID belonging to another workspace can POST it back (optionally with a workspaceId), overwriting the existing record and potentially reassigning it to the attacker's workspace, since the service never verifies that the existing object belongs to the caller before saving (broken object-level authorization / cross-workspace takeover).
